about:
As Co-founder of Avalor Will is responsible for building an outstanding team of scientific business and clinical development experts to make Avalor a leader in addressing major unmet medical needs driven by chronic inflammation. 

Prior to Avalor Will started Nanotech Bio in 2012 a startup that pioneered the development of graphene bio-electronic sensing technology (GBEST) for drug discovery. Nanotech Bio collaborated with Amgen to enable and demonstrate lead discovery and target engagement characterization with challenging integral membrane proteins like SLC transporters. GBEST was licensed early to Amgen. Prior to Nanotech Bio Will worked at Atomica (formerly IMT) where he led the early development of transformative biosensor products like PacBio’s SMRT Cell® which has become a dominant commercial product in long-read DNA sequencing & precision medicine (NASDAQ:PACB).

Will was born in a Copper mining village in Peru. Through merit-based scholarships he immigrated to the US and obtained his B.S. and M.S. degrees in Materials Engineering from Colorado School of Mines & UC Santa Barbara respectively.

company_description:
Coprata brings innovation to gut health monitoring enabling personalized wellness and enhancing research. 
Founded by experienced scientists and engineers from Duke University Coprata blends rigorous research with user-centered design to create wellness tools that are both scientifically sound and accessible for everyday use.
Our Microbiome Activity Tracker (MAT) is a rapid at-home stool test that turns complex microbiome science into a simple score empowering users to stop guessing and start measuring the impact of their choices on their gut health. MAT is designed for people seeking to prevent chronic disease optimize wellness or manage existing health conditions through diet and lifestyle.
Coprata's Smart Toilet provides automated non-invasive biomarker tracking and leverages a unique hands-free stool sampling technology that has been piloted in real bathrooms.

Equilibar offers revolutionary fluid control for the most challenging applications in research biotech and industry. Our engineers have expertise in multi-phase flow supercritical states automation biopharma standards continuous flow and severe service.

Equilibar® patented devices provide back pressure control vacuum control and flow control. They are available in a range of materials including single use options. 

EQUILIBAR BACK PRESSURE REGULATORS have proven their value for more than 15 years with solid performance in fuel cell research oil and gas supercritical pharmaceutical engineering and other cutting edge industries.

EQUILIBAR'S VACUUM CONTROL VALVES have been used for NASA's Artemis Mission and by the US Navy’s Altitude Laboratory at the Naval Air Warfare Center where they played a key role in an application that required stable pressure across flow rate changes of up to 1 000 to 1.

THE EQUILIBAR FLOW CONTROL VALVE offers a wide Cv range chemical compatibility and multiphase fluid handling. This device can provide a solution for unique scenarios such as complex oil well injection applications that are beyond the capabilities of traditional valves.

For upstream and downstream biopharmaceutical applications with multiple processing needs our Single Use Technology (SUT) Division offers a suite of products that meet Pharma 4.0® guidelines.

Equilibar's core technology features a dome-loaded multiple orifice design that is radically different from conventional valves and regulators. Its synergistic design elements result in high sensitivity 1000:1 flow rate turn-down ratio easy automation for computer control extreme precision and virtually instantaneous reaction time. 

Since May of 2021 Equilibar has been part of the Richards Industrials Family. Equilibar’s Quality System is ISO 9001:2015 Certified.

We are proud to work and manufacture in the United States and honored to partner with innovators around the world.

about:
My current research topics include the following:
(1) Understanding RNA structure conformational heterogeneity and RNA-RNA interactions during viral replication.
(2) Analysis of SARS-CoV-2 sequence variation with a particular focus on “silent” mutations for determining the importance of RNA structures.
(3) Use of both biochemical/structure techniques (chromatography multi-angle light scattering small-angle X-ray scattering cryo-electron microscopy) and computational methods (sequence analysis and molecular modeling) to understand the molecular structure and mechanism of RNA-dependent RNA-polymerase initiation during SARS-CoV-2 replication.
(4) Developing novel methods of determining secondary and tertiary structures of RNA and understanding the role of conformational heterogeneity in RNA function.

about:
Professor Fungal Pathogen Researcher Genomics & Bioinformatics Expert

I'm a Professor of Biological Sciences at Northern Arizona University and a researcher at the Pathogen and Microbiome Institute with a passion for understanding and combating fungal diseases—especially Coccidioides spp. the pathogens behind Valley Fever.

With a background in genetics microbiology and computational biology my work bridges molecular research and real-world health challenges. I lead projects focused on fungal genomics host-pathogen interactions and environmental surveillance and I direct NAU's ABSL-3 lab where we're pioneering in vivo transcriptomics and CRISPR-based functional genomics.

My research has been supported by NIH (R21 K22 U19) and I collaborate on initiatives to improve diagnostics develop vaccines and better understand the ecological and genetic factors driving fungal virulence and distribution.

Let's connect if you're interested in infectious disease research fungal biology or collaborative science that spans the lab bench to public health impact.

See my pubs at https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/myncbi/bridget.barker.1/bibliography/public/

### Selected Recent Publications

 **Tenório BG Kollath DR et al. Barker BM.** (2024). *Tracing histoplasmosis genomic epidemiology and species occurrence across the USA.* **Emerging Microbes & Infections** 
→ Mapped the genomic landscape of *Histoplasma* revealing new insights into its spread and diversity.

 **Eulálio KD Kollath DR et al. Barker BM.** (2024). *Epidemiological clinical and genomic landscape of coccidioidomycosis in northeastern Brazil.* **Nature Communications** 
→ First large-scale genomic study of Valley Fever in Brazil highlighting regional emergence and diversity.

 **Coyne VU Mead HLG Mongini PKAVS Barker BM.** (2023). *B Cell Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia Development in Mice with Chronic Lung Exposure to Coccidioides.* **ImmunoHorizons** 
→ Uncovered potential links between chronic fungal exposure and hematologic malignancies.

 **McHardy IH Barker BM Thompson GR.** (2023). *Review of Clinical and Laboratory Diagnostics for Coccidioidomycosis.* **Journal of Clinical Microbiology** 
→ A go-to reference for clinicians and researchers on current diagnostic tools for Valley Fever.

I have always been an avid maker and technology enthusiast having cofounded a state of the art manufacturing company in Ohio at a young age. My career in medical technology began at Columbia University where I first marveled at the vast network of biochemical signals that regulate our bodies. I was concurrently working in New York City hospitals and was astonished at the realization that even today nearly all of these signals cannot be observed or monitored in real-time.

These experiences led to a deep interest in developing biosensors to provide insight into today’s diseases by more directly monitoring these signals. Today I am the founder and CEO of AptaScan a Silicon Valley startup creating a novel biosensor for real-time continuous monitoring applications. Our sensor will first be applied to detecting imminent cytokine storms which are acute and deadly complications triggered by many diseases including sepsis influenza and Ebola.

company_description:
The University of Maryland School of Medicine was chartered in 1807 as the first public medical school in the United States. It continues today as one of the fastest growing top-tier biomedical research enterprises in the world -- with 43 academic departments centers institutes and programs and a faculty of more than 3 000 physicians scientists and allied health professionals including members of the National Academies of Science Engineering and Medicine and a distinguished recipient of the Albert E. Lasker Award in Medical Research. 

With an operating budget of more than $1 billion the School of Medicine works closely in partnership with the University of Maryland Medical Center and Medical System to provide research-intensive academic and clinically-based care for more than 1.2 million patients each year. The School has more than 2 500 students residents and fellows and nearly $450 million in extramural funding with more than half of its academic departments ranked in the top 20 among all public medical schools in the nation in research funding.

As one of the seven professional schools that make up the University of Maryland Baltimore campus the School of Medicine has a total workforce of nearly 7 000 individuals. The combined School and Medical System ("University of Maryland Medicine") has a total budget of $5 billion and an economic impact of nearly $15 billion on the state and local community. The School of Medicine faculty which ranks as the 8th-highest public medical school in research productivity is an innovator in translational medicine with 600 active patents and 24 start-up companies. The School works locally nationally and globally with research and treatment facilities in 36 countries around the world. 

The University of Maryland School of Medicine has three research institutes: the Institute of Human Virology (IHV) the Institute for Genome Sciences (IGS) and the Institute for Global Health (IGH).

about:
My research interests center on the development of sensors for accurate chemical measurements in complex mixtures. One focus is the measurement of glucose in the body for the management of diabetes. Our approach involves passing a band of harmless near infrared light through a fold of skin and determining the concentration of glucose from an analysis of the resulting spectrum. Attractive features of this approach are that it is reagentless painless and unaffected by the foreign body response that plagues implantable biosensor for continuous glucose measurements. 

Time-domain terahertz (THz) spectroscopy is also being developed for characterization of new materials and novel analytical applications in gaseous and solid matrixes. 

Specialties: Near infrared spectroscopy THz spectroscopy Multivariate analysis Noninvasive glucose monitoring Bioprocess monitoring and ICU monitoring.

I am a multi-skilled R&D Engineer happiest when tinkering possibilities into unique solutions. Specializing in chem/bio-sensor development scientific device prototyping and industrial design.

In my youth I spent my free time thinking about “the possibilities ” dreaming up new contraptions and putting them to paper. I would accompany my dad on his trips to the local landfill to help him toss our waste material only to return to my dad’s annoyance with discarded parts I had found to assemble my own toys (I miss that dual ceramic insulator “phaser pistol”). 

 I still get excited thinking about “the possibilities” and it has enticed me to lead or collaborate with other researchers in a diverse range of investigations—from distributed chemical sensor arrays and their use in IoT to printing viable wound tissue samples in sub-zero temperatures. The majority of my efforts were dedicated to unique chem/bio sensor detection technology sponsored by agencies such as NIH ONR DARPA DTRA DHS and the IC. 

Insights I gained while attempting to effectively demonstrate our benchtop prototypes to potential stakeholders led me to one of my major contributions: establishing a 3D prototyping facility. Here unique apparatuses were designed and fabricated that ultimately allowed my colleagues to effectively showcase their state-of-the-art technology to business and military VIPs and be highly competitive in obtaining new or follow-on multi-million dollar funding.

As PI I was able to build a strong rapport with my teams by advising establishing clear objectives finding multiple solutions to a challenge and participating in our investigative efforts with my computer aided manufacturing and electronics instrumentation background. I efficiently managed budgets exceeding $1 million and provided written and oral reports of our progress to our sponsors.

 Presently I am part of a remarkable team at Theia Scientific LLC that are thought leaders in Edge Computing AI and Machine Learning. My role is multidimensional and relies on my experience in: 

 Instrumentation R&D Software programming Microscopy automation Bio-sensing applications Industrial design Grant proposal writing Project management Formulating creative R&D options

 “The possibilities” continue to propel my evolving career.

company_description:
Breakthru Medicine focuses on aggressive solid tumors with the highest unmet need—cancers where patients have exhausted existing options. By attacking the specific molecular drivers of each tumor we design precision therapies for patients whose cancers have become resistant to current treatments.

Breakthru Medicine is a privately held preclinical biotechnology company founded by industry veterans with over a dozen FDA approvals between them— and a majority with breakthrough therapy designations. We're building multiple independent scientific platforms each designed to crack a different class of previously untreatable cancers. The Breakthru team has spent decades solving medicinal chemistry problems that others walked away from turning "undruggable" targets into precision medicines.

The Breakthru team is not just good at making drugs. We're exceptional at knowing which drugs not to make. We've learned that the strongest teams exercise data-driven brutal honesty about when a compound isn't likely to become a breakthrough medicine—and move on efficiently. This pattern recognition only comes from years of drug development experience—both our successes and our scars.

Though preclinical we know what winning looks like and what chasing the wrong target costs. For patients told "There are no more options " we're building the next ones.
headline: Cancer drug developer and oncology executive

MT Explorer 30 is a small form factor instrument that boasts performance comparable to laboratory benchtop 3D trap instruments. current_position_description: My lab conducts time-course studies of brain dynamics in rodent models of traumatic brain injury stroke and epilepsy for preclinical evaluation of novel therapeutics and to identify new therapeutic targets. We develop and use implantable micro-optic devices and biosensors for longitudinal monitoring of changes in morphology and function of fluorescently labeled cells in deep brain regions of rodents and to capture dynamics of neurotransmitter release. Our temporal resolution ranges from a fraction of a second to months. We image and record from animals prior to induction of injury or disease and then record from the same animals over time after induction and treatment. Thus the animals serve as their own baseline controls reducing experimental variability. Our upcoming work will combine implanted EEG electrodes and neurochemical sensors with implanted micro-optics to more fully understand the physiology of cells in targeted brain regions. Behavioral experiments are also performed in the same animals. This led us to develop novel automated methods to analyze behavioral experiments. PUBLICATIONS: http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sites/myncbi/collections/bibliography/48510492/

about: Isaac Macwan PhD joined Fairfield University School of Engineering & Computing in September 2019. He received his PhD from the University of Bridgeport in 2014. He received his Master of Science degree in Electrical Engineering from the University of Bridgeport and Bachelor of Engineering degree in Electrical Engineering from the University of Pune India. He teaches courses in design and engineering of biomedical systems and devices bio and nano electronics and dynamics of bio- and nano molecular systems. Dr. Macwan’s current research interest is in bioelectronics biomaterials and molecular dynamics with applications in biosignal detection and transduction micro and nano scaffolds for biosensors and molecular interactions at the interface of biological macromolecules and carbon-based nanoparticles. This interdisciplinary work involves the diverse fields of electrical engineering bioengineering and certain aspects of computer science. The future goal of the Macwan group is to be able to synthesize biocompatible scaffolds for disease diagnosis and understand the atomic level interactions between biological and synthetic polymers.

about:
Patrick Blake is the Founder and President of Sophic Alliance Inc. with over 40 years of experience leading innovations in healthcare and life sciences. A recognized thought leader entrepreneur author and proud veteran of the Marine Corps Patrick has pioneered cutting-edge scientific and technological solutions that have shaped advancements across the industry. His work reflects a deep commitment to transforming complex challenges into actionable data-driven outcomes.
 
Patrick has over 45 years of software and consulting experience in life science and has delivered a range of solutions to government and commercial customers. Under his leadership Sophic teamed with Biomax Informatics to deliver major oncology projects for the National Cancer Institute. Sophic’s advanced technology and scientific excellence earned two competitive SBIR (Small Business Innovation Research) Awards. 

Patrick is the author of two critically acclaimed novels. “The Cross of Gallantry” is a story about Marines in Vietnam who sacrificed fought and died during combat operations on the DMZ in 1967-68. “Ward F – Sequel to The Cross of Gallantry” is a story about Marines wounded in battle their struggles to recover from life changing injuries the Navy doctors nurses and corpsmen who cared for them and the hostile “welcome home” they received from an ungrateful nation.

Patrick attended New York Institute of Technology majoring in Public Communications. He served in the U.S. Marine Corps from 1964-75 and was a combat Marine in Vietnam from 1967-68 where he earned both personal and unit combat medals.

Founded in 2000 and headquartered in Barcelona Spain Oryzon brings together a highly experienced team accross Barcelona Boston and New Jersey.

The Company's clinical portfolio is built around two differentiated LSD1 inhibitors. Iadademstat its oncology/hematology program is being evaluated in multiple Phase I and II clinical trials with very promising efficacy signals in first-line acute myeloid leukemia including a 100% overall response rate (ORR) presented at ASH 2025 with additional data expected at EHA 2026. 

Vafidemstat its lead CNS candidate is in clinical development across neuropsychiatric indications including aggression in borderline personality disorder (Phase III in preparation) negative symptoms of schizophrenia (ongoing Phase IIb) and aggression in autism spectrum disorder (ASD) including genetically defined populations such as Phelan-McDermid syndrome (Phase II in preparation).

Beyond LSD1 Oryzon is expanding its pipeline to new targets such as HDAC6 with ORY-4001 nominated for potential development in neurological diseases including ALS and CMT.

Oryzon also leverages a robust biomarker platform supporting precision medicine approaches. The Company is listed on the Spanish Stock Exchange (ORY) and has attracted specialized international investors from the United States and Europe.

about: As an Assistant Professor I have been involved in conducting research and teaching courses in the Department of Life and Physical Sciences in the School of Natural Sciences Mathematics and Business at Fisk University. I also serve as the Bioengineering Program Coordinator. I am a mentor in the Fisk-Vanderbilt Master's-to-PhD Bridge Program and the Fisk-University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (UIUC) which is also called the FUTURE-MINDS-QB Program. As an adjoint professor in the Department of Biomedical Engineering at Vanderbilt University I help mentor and train students while using world-class research facilities to conduct research in the area of surface acoustic wave biosensor development. Currently I collaborate with a number of research faculty national laboratories and industrial partners in this area.
current_position_description:
Assistant Professor of Bioengineering
As an Assistant Professor at Fisk University I am developing an ultra-sensitive handheld shear-horizontal surface acoustic wave (SAW) biosensor to detect viral pathogens using antigen testing rapidly. A similar SAW biosensor was used to successfully detect HIV I/II viral antigens from clinical serum samples. However the concentrations detected are too high and therefore impractical for application for clinical screening of patients. The variability in test results leads to considerable variances among samples. High variances are partially due to low signal-to-noise ratios and chip-to-chip variability. Here we propose modifying existing gold-coated sensors to improve the sensor sensitivity and overall signal-to-noise ratio. A technology gap that limits these devices' accuracy and precision directly contributes to high overall variance. The primary goal of this project is to improve current technology by enhancing the signal and overall signal-to-noise ratio by adding a polymer acoustic waveguide to a gold. Lowering the noise level due to the biological matrices will lower the overall noise floor recorded by the biosensor and improve sensitivity accuracy and precision.

about:

As a Research Consultant at the UC Institute for Prediction Technology (UCIPT) I apply my expertise in biosensor design data analysis and artificial intelligence to create innovative solutions that leverage social big data for predictive modeling of human behaviors and outcomes.

In collaboration with multidisciplinary teams of psychologists computer scientists and mathematicians I have contributed to several projects that aim to understand and improve the health safety and well-being of individuals and communities. Some of my achievements include designing a wristwatch that monitors vital signs and emotional states developing a system that measures drivers' pleasure and fatigue and publishing a book that explores the ethical challenges of placebo effects. I am driven by the vision of creating emotionally-sensitive machines that can enhance the quality of life and the performance of people in various domains and scenarios.

about:
I am an entrepreneurial minded Researcher and Scientist with interests and experience in gene editing recombinant protein expression in mammalian cells molecular biology and stem cells. Throughout my 15+ years as a researcher I have learned and applied cutting-edge techniques including CRISPR-Cas9 proteomics and bioinformatics. 

Some of my lab's recent work includes the development of a patented technology (FAST HDR) which uses CRISPR gene-editing to tag up to 3 genes within a cell allowing for live-cell microscopical analysis (no fixing or staining required) to better understand natural cell processes or drug effectiveness in-vitro without altering the cell's physiological environment.

My ultimate passion lies in aiding drug discovery and in particular 
how the knowledge and understanding of the human genome allows the move towards personalized medicine - which I teach about via my Pharmacogenomics course.

about:
I am an entrepreneur researcher and AI hardware architect focused on turning unconventional technical ideas into commercially viable products.

My current interest is in developing the next generation of unconventional AI hardware—architectures that move beyond the limitations of conventional digital GPUs and address the growing challenges of power consumption memory bandwidth data movement and data-center infrastructure. I am particularly interested in analog and in-memory computing neuromorphic and spiking systems novel memory technologies chiplet architectures and tightly integrated hardware–algorithm co-design for large AI models.

I have been working on deep-learning accelerators since the early development of the field and have designed and implemented more than five generations of AI accelerator architectures. My work spans neural-network architecture design model optimization AI processors edge AI data-center inference multimodal learning large language models robotics digital health and autonomous scientific discovery.

I am at my best when leading small highly capable teams pursuing ideas that larger organizations may consider too unconventional difficult or early. My experience includes building companies and technical teams raising capital developing investment and commercialization strategies licensing technology negotiating partnerships mentoring engineers and founders managing M&A processes and working with organizations ranging from startups to Fortune 500 companies.

In 2017 I founded FWDNXT a company developing deep-learning accelerators machine-learning hardware and AI solutions. Our team built the company through product development and commercialization culminating in its acquisition by Micron Technology.

In 2014 I founded TeraDeep where I led the company through more than $1.5 million in investment strategic partnerships and the development of deep-learning products for intelligent cameras and edge systems.
I received a Laurea degree in Electronic Engineering from the University of Trieste an M.S. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Johns Hopkins University and a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from Johns Hopkins University.

My honors include the Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers and recognition as an IEEE Circuits and Systems Society Distinguished Lecturer. I have authored more than 150 research papers and two books.

about:
Dr. John T. McDevitt has a strong track record of translating essential bioscience discoveries into real-world clinical practice. In this capacity he serves as the Scientific Founder for several diagnostic companies. His most recent company OraLiva features clinical services and decision support tools capable of capturing diseases early before they spiral out of control.

McDevitt also serves as a Professor for the Department Biomaterials at New York University College of Dentistry is a member of NYU’s Bioengineering Institute and participates as a faculty member in the NYU Department of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering within the Tandon School of Engineering. McDevitt and his team over the past decade have raised over $30M in Federal and Foundation support. His recent research has been sponsored by major programs funded by the NIH the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation Cancer Prevention Research Institute of Texas NASA and the United Kingdom’s Home Office Scientific Development Branch.

McDevitt and his team have written more than 200 peer-reviewed scientific manuscripts and have contributed to more than 100 patents and patent applications. This work was recognized with “2016 AACC Wallace H. Coulter Lectureship Award ” “Best of What's New Award” in the Medical Device Category for 2008 by Popular Science as well as for the “Best Scientific Advances Award” in 1998 by the Science Coalition. Dr. McDevitt’s individual honors include the Presidential Young Investigator Award the 2010 California Polytechnic Distinguished Alumni Award and the Exxon Education Award. Over the past 7 years Dr. McDevitt has served as the Principal Investigator for 6 major clinical trials and 2 clinical pilot studies all involving the programmable bio-nano-chip. Through these clinical efforts mini-sensor ensembles are being developed for major diseases in the areas of oral cancer cardiac heart disease trauma drugs of abuse ovarian cancer and prostate cancer.

The team is focused on the development of medical micro-devices that have the potential to have a strong impact on the delivery of healthcare. The interests of the McDevitt group span from nanoscience & nanoengineering through immunology chemistry biology physics and move onto global health issues. The team's goal is to move past the publications into development of distributed tools that impact the practice of medicine on a global basis. The McDevitt lab not only develops state-of-the-art diagnostic devices but we customize and validate them in the context of real-world clinical trials and partner with organizations that will place these devices into widespread clinical practice.
company_description: OraLiva is a diagnostic company pioneering a new era of non-invasive AI-powered cytology ultimately designed for use directly at the point of care. OraLiva's proprietary technology platform combines state-of-the-art biosensing with AI to analyze single-cell data from a simple brush sample. Within minutes the test detects key cytologic signatures of dysplasia—the cellular warning signs that precede cancer—providing providers with lab-grade insight in real time. OraLiva's mission is to eliminate delays in cancer diagnosis and make lifesaving precision diagnostics accessible to every patient everywhere.

about: Dr. Coskun is a Bernie-Marcus Early-Career Professor of Biomedical Engineering at Georgia Institute of Technology and Emory University. Dr. Coskun is a systems biotechnologist and bioengineer working at the nexus of multiplexed cell imaging and quantitative tissue biology. Dr. Coskun directs an interdisciplinary research team at the Single Cell Biotechnology and Spatial Omics Laboratory an interdisciplinary program strategically positioned for multiparameter imaging one cell at a time by spatial context and function. Dr. Coskun holds 5 issued patents and is also the co-author of more than 50 peer-reviewed publications in major scientific journals. He is a recipient of the NSF CAREER Award 2024 NIH R35 MIRA Award 2023 BMES-CMBE Rising Star Award 2023 American Lung Association Innovation Award 2022 and Student Recognition of Excellence in Teaching: Class of 1934 CIOS Award among other research and teaching awards. Previously Dr. Coskun was an Instructor at Stanford University. Dr. Coskun received his postdoctoral training from the California Institute of Technology. He holds a Ph.D. degree from the University of California Los Angeles.His research has been supported by Federal and Private grants including the National Institutes of Health (NIA NIAID NCI NIDCR OD and ORIP) Wellcome LEAP Burroughs Wellcome Fund (CASI) NSF CMaT American Cancer Society IRG Multi-cellular engineered living systems (M-CELS) and Regenerative Medicine Center. In addition he leads outreach programs to engage K12 students and undergraduate students through BioCrowd Studio an innovative crowd-sourcing program bringing together interactive virtual media distributed biokits and collaborative spatial discovery.

about: Dr. Kiefer began his career with the Dow Chemical Company working in Discovery Research where he led in the development of innovative chelation chemistry technologies for diagnostic and therapeutic medicine. During his 20 years with Dow he played a key role in the strategic growth and implementation of chelating agent platforms that were instrumental for the company’s medical chelation programs. His major accomplishments include the design of DOTA-based ligands for nuclear medicine applications leading the development of innovative diagnostic targeted contrast agent technologies for magnetic resonance and optical imaging and discovery of the mechanism for targeting lanthanide chelates to cancer cells through structure/activity relationships. He was recognized as the Dow Gulf Coast Scientist of the year on two occasions (1993 and 1999) and as the Dow Gulf Coast Inventor of the Year in 1996. In 2005 Dr. Kiefer retired from Dow and assumed his current position with Macrocyclics. Since that time he has focused on expanding the company’s GMP capabilities and new product offerings as well as strategic partnerships. In addition to his technical accomplishments Dr. Kiefer remains active in the supervision of external collaborators and management of intellectual assets and commercial strategies. Most recently Dr. Kiefer played a strategic role in identifying and facilitating the partnership which led to the acquisition of Macrocyclics by Orano MED in 2011. Dr. Kiefer also holds an appointment as Research Professor of Chemistry at the University of Texas at Dallas. His research interests include the architectural design of targeted chelates that can function as physiological probes. Dr. Kiefer has published over 100 peer reviewed articles and 40 patents.

Tissue X is an innovative technology in that macromolecules that couldn’t be delivered to the epidermis(inner skin) by conventional cosmetics - such as hyaluronic acid(HA) and Argireline - can be successfully and stably delivered. Also API's that could be delivered only through subcutaneous injection such as exenatide and teriparatide can now be delivered through this small sized patch. 

Biosensor Lab is also developing an electro-chemistry POCT(Point of Care Testing) technology that provides an effective sensor solution to pool management and food safety.

about:
Dr. de Mora is an accomplished scientist engineer and medical researcher with over a decade of experience developing diverse medical and scientific projects that deliver solutions to create impact and address global challenges.

Dr. de Mora has developed multiple medical technologies and devices during his career. Dr. de Mora worked on the initial version of an innovative cartridge-based renal dialysis system to enable home treatment and improve quality of care for renal dialysis patients. The project eventually became Quanta Dialysis a home hemodialysis console and was granted an FDA 510(k) clearance in December 2020. 

Dr. de Mora was previously the Director of Development for the International Genetically Engineered Machines (iGEM) Foundation. Dr. de Mora assembled a team of 165 judges to evaluate more than 300 teams from 42 countries. The competition has resulted in over 150 companies including several with valuations over $1 Billion such as Ginkgo Bioworks Benchling and OpenTrons.

Dr. de Mora led the development of an arsenic biosensor system that won multiple prizes during the 2006 iGEM competition. His work was profiled in Wired Magazine and was described in several books on the origins of the field of synthetic biology including Biology is Technology by Rob Carlson and Synthetic Aesthetics by Alexandra Daisy Ginsberg. Dr. de Mora’s work inspired iGEM teams to address major global challenges including developing technologies that address plastic degradation in the oceans combat declining honey bee populations and rapidly detect pathogenic fungal infections.

Dr. de Mora worked on the development of BioBrick genetic regulatory measurement techniques during his Ph.D. This work eventually became the foundation for the largest interlab measurement study in the field of synthetic biology with participants from over 200 labs worldwide. During his postdoctoral research he further developed an ex vivo zebrafish heart culture model and created a high-throughput histological sectioning technique.

Dr. de Mora was selected for the Synthetic Biology Leadership Accelerator (Synbio LEAP) Fellow was
awarded a Lessells Travel Scholarship from the Royal Society of Edinburgh to visit Harvard Medical School and had his career profiled in Science Magazine. 

Dr. de Mora holds a Masters in Mechanical Engineering and Management from the University of Edinburgh. Dr. de Mora was also the first person in the United Kingdom to be awarded a Ph.D. in Synthetic Biology based on work done with BioBricks also from the University of Edinburgh.

about:
Avails Medical is a pioneer in rapid automated all-electrical antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST). Founded by a team of Stanford post-docs Avails’ electronic biosensor technology is designed to improve speed and accuracy in pathogen quantification and susceptibility testing directly from human specimens by eliminating crude time-consuming culturing steps.
Avails eQUANT technology enables a single-step automated 0.5 McFarland preparation (in as fast as 40 minutes) directly from positive blood culture (PBC) removing 18+ hours from workflow by eliminating the need for subcultures. eQUANT enables legacy AST systems to be used directly from PBC extending their useful life by 10 – 15 years. eQUANT also allows for direct-from-PBC disk diffusion.
Avails is developing a rapid phenotypic AST (eAST) for use directly from PBC as well as cultured isolates with 90% faster results for antibiotic therapy. eAST will be a critical step forward in improving accurate and efficient antibiotic therapy decisions in multi-drug resistant infections.
Avails’ electrical biosensor platform fits within a lab’s existing workflow and budget. Its modular development enables maximum flexibility from single-slot systems to any number of multi-slot systems serving all market segments from smaller community hospitals to larger academic labs.
company_description:
Avails Medical Inc. is a pioneer in rapid automated solutions for antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST). Founded by a team of Stanford postdoctoral researchers Avails' electronic biosensor technology is designed to improve affordability and speed in pathogen quantification and susceptibility testing directly from human specimens for clinical laboratories by eliminating manual time-consuming culturing steps.
 
Avails' eQUANT(TM) platform enables microbiology labs to generate an automated 0.5 McFarland equivalent (eMcF) directly from positive blood cultures (PBC) removing as many as 18+ hours from the standard workflow by eliminating the need for subcultures. 

The eQUANT system is FDA 510(k) cleared.
 
IN DEVELOPMENT: Avails is also developing a rapid phenotypic AST platform being designed for use directly from PBCs as well as cultured isolates. 

Avails' electrical biosensor platform fits within the laboratory's existing workflow and budget serving clinical labs in smaller community hospitals to larger academic centers and centralized laboratories.

about:
Five Vital Signs (5VS) is committed to providing Lifesaving Triage & Medical Awareness in real time anywhere on the planet. 5VS will save lives increase the quality of life and decrease the cost of healthcare. John is a board certified Emergency Room physician who has worked for years in high volume high acuity Level II trauma centers. Given his background with the military and medicine 5VS reflects his commitment to make a contribution to our United States Armed Forces.

John graduated from the United States Naval Academy (BS) the University of Texas in Austin (MBA) the University of Texas Medical Branch in Galveston (MD) and the Louisiana State University Emergency Medicine Program in Baton Rouge.
current_position_description:
5VS began with and continues with the determination to save the lives of wounded American soldiers by allowing the medic or any soldier within one hundred yards to identify and prioritize the care of anyone critically wounded on the battlefield. 
Triage-On-Demand (TOD) is an 11 gram credit card sized biosensor which attaches to the arm. It utilizes Photoplethysmography (PPG) technology and is rechargeable. TOD captures and relays vital signs in real time. More specifically it captures Heart Rate Respiratory Rate Body Temperature as well as Position/Fall Detection. Additionally it serves as an advanced warning system identifying vital sign abnormalities/concerns given a soldier's age along with their co-morbidities such as diabetes hypertension and elevated cholesterol. Lastly with a high degree of probability TOD will detect Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) Orthostatic Hypotension and a Pulsed Electromagnetic attack (Havana Syndrome) whether the attack is a few feet or hundreds of miles in diameter. TOD is comfortable the metrics are reliable and the accuracy of the vital signs are not affected by vasoconstriction secondary to hemorrhage dehydration or sepsis.

www.fivevitalsigns.com
current_position_2_description: High volume high acuity Emergency Departments in Louisiana Tennessee and Texas.

about: Natesh is CEO and Co-Founder of Khufu Therapeutics an IP-backed seed stage California-based (UCSD-HomeLab) synthetic biology organ engineering company biotechnology company that reimagines therapy for patients destined to die from severe liver disease. Natesh is an MIT/Harvard/Stanford/UCSF trained physician scientist /bioengineer former surgeon tenure track professor and entrepreneur. As an undergrad (MIT) Natesh researched the bioartificial liver (liver tissue engineering) (Harvard Med) for over two years. Next he trained to be a liver transplant surgeon (BU). Later he pursued a doctorate (Ph.D.) to investigate liver differentiation with pluripotent stem cells (PSC) (Rutgers (Coursework) Harvard Med (research)). Next Natesh leveraged his broad expertise to develop non-invasive imaging tools to study PSC differentiation in vivo. He studied Molecular Imaging under the world's expert (Stanford) studying in vivo imaging of stem cells for differentiation cell therapy and organogenesis. Next Natesh obtained a California Institute of Regenerative Medicine (CIRM) fellowship to investigate human PSCs and early organ development (UCSF). Later as a tenure track professor (State University of New York) Natesh ran a large research group focused on liver organogenesis answering how the liver achieves its massive size and how we can reverse engineer stem cells to build liver tissue. He developed hepatic differentiation technology organogenesis approaches hepatic organoid technology and computational/AI analysis of differentiation for stem cells aging and cancer.Natesh produced 32 peer-reviewed publications and 3 Conference Proceedings with 46 technical presentations and 68 conference presentations at numerous academic engineering biology and medicine conferences. Natesh’s dream has been apply technology to the clinic and so he founded Khufu Therapeutics in late 2022. Prior to this he participated in entrepreneur training programs at MIT the Stanford Biodesign program the UCSF entrepreneurship program NSF ZAP and NSF I-Corps programs. Additionally he was founding partner at New League Capital a Silicon Valley accelerator (2012) where he was in charge of Life Science Companies and co-founded Vicara Capital (2013-2016) an early-stage pre-seed fund for science-based startups. Most recently he rejected a $500K pre-seed investment from a San Francisco firm and obtained a $585K NSF grant. He participated in UCSD HomeLab Accelerator UC Foundry program for 14 weeks to refine his CEO training and ensure Khufu's success.

company_description:
Who are we?

ASML is an innovation leader in the global semiconductor industry. We make machines that chipmakers use to mass produce microchips. Founded in 1984 in the Netherlands with just a handful of employees we've now grown to over 40 000 employees 143 nationalities and more than 60 locations around the world.

What do we do?

We provide chipmakers with hardware software and services to mass produce patterns on silicon through lithography. Our lithography systems use ultraviolet light to create billions of tiny structures on silicon that together make up a microchip. We push our technology to new limits to enable our customers to create smaller faster and more powerful chips.

Who are our people?

While you may think that only engineers and mathematicians work at ASML you'll be surprised to find out that our people come from a wide variety of backgrounds. Across ASML we have dedicated teams that manage customer support communications and media IT software development and more. Every team in the company is essential for pushing our technology and the industry forward.

If you love to tackle challenges and innovate in a collaborative supportive and inclusive environment with all the flexibility and freedom to unleash your full potential ASML is the place to be.

Join us!

about:
Our research program is focused on enabling technologies for human-machine hybrid cardiac tissue using 3D bioprinting technology to assemble complex arrays of interfaces between synthetic and biological materials. Currently we are focusing on 3D bioprinting of hiPSC-derived cardiomyocytes and endothelial cells into pre-vascularized functional cardiac tissue constructs for the use as in vitro models of cardiac disease and development and to develop new cardiac regenerative therapies in vivo. 

In addition we conduct research in neonatal cardiac development and repair (my NIH K99-R00 project) with the aim to develop new regenerative approaches – based on cardiac patch progenitor cell and 3D bioprinting technology – to treat ischemic heart injury in neonatal and adult mammalian heart. Our central hypothesis is that a cell-based regenerative response is present in the neonatal heart that can be recruited via a bioprinted cardiac patch delivery of growth factors/small molecules for the treatment of myocardial injury.

Finally we also have projects in high-throughput drug screening concentrating on the use of 3D bioprinted patient-specific platforms for high-throughput screening of arrhythmogenic pharmaceutical drugs.

company_description:
Quantapore’s novel high throughput massively parallel sequencer will allow rapid affordable and sensitive sequencing of entire genomes. This desktop system will address the need for efficient sequencing. Revenues will be garnered by seeding units into customer facilities and then by the sale of consumable cartridges providing rapid growth as the installed base and customer utilization increases. 

Quantapore’s novel proprietary DNA sequencing technology is based on an optically labeled nanopore which interacts via energy transfer with a labeled nucleic acid sample. The quantum-optic interaction between the nanopore and the DNA allows for single molecule nucleic acid sequencing at an unprecedented speed and cost. 

The global market for genetics/genomics is estimated at $5.8 billion while the DNA sequencing segment is estimated at ~$2.7 billion. The sequencing market will approximately double by 2015 with equipment and consumable sales representing around 2/3 of the total market. However the un-tapped potential of this market is currently estimated at about $30 billion leaving a large unmet demand for sequencing.
headline: Director at Quantapore Inc.

1. Tissue engineering approaches to understanding and modulating the growth aging and injury responses of the retina to oxidative stress.

3. Nanotechnology and Biomedical Engineering: the application of electrochemical sensors and nanotechnology to clinical medicine and surgery and the development of novel ocular surgical instrument designs based upon new biomaterials and nanofabrication techniques.

George Serafin currently serves as President and CEO of NanoDx Inc. a privately held medical device company developing innovative rapid near-patient point-of-care in-vitro diagnostic solutions on its proprietary nanowire biosensor platform to address unmet market needs improve patient outcomes and reduce healthcare costs. George is a seasoned professional possessing a unique blend of industry technology and management consulting experience spanning R&D engineering supply chain manufacturing quality regulatory and information technology across medical device and diagnostics pharmaceuticals biotechnology and consumer healthcare.

With his broad and deep knowledge and “concept-to-patient” perspective George has worked with Top-Ten to pre-revenue companies spanning late-stage development through commercialization and post-market surveillance. He has assisted clients globally (having worked in five continents) in the development and execution of business strategies that promote innovation and growth operational efficiency and process excellence and sustained regulatory compliance.

George is a strategic advisor to the FDA assisting them in various areas including product quality patient safety advanced manufacturing data integrity cloud computing and digital health. Most recently he has been supporting FDA’s Case for Quality Initiative having co-led several projects with the FDA and the Medical Device Innovation Consortium (MDIC) including the Voluntary Improvement Program.

He is recognized as a subject matter expert and has testified to the United States International Trade Commission (USITC) and Federal Trade Commission (FTC).

Most recently George served as FTI Consulting's life sciences industry leader responsible for the integrated delivery of services and solutions for clients across the life sciences sector including pharmaceutical biotechnology medical technology and consumer healthcare.

Before joining FTI George served in various national and global leadership roles at Grant Thornton and Deloitte & Touche while always serving clients as his top priority. He started his career at Novartis in R&D and then progressed across the enterprise value chain with increasing responsibility in engineering manufacturing/operations quality and regulatory roles working for Roche and Fresenius Medical Care. Subsequently he transitioned to the technology sector leading the development of SAP’s life sciences solution through several critical releases.

company_description:
NanoDx Inc. is a privately-held U.S. based medical device company developing rapid point-of-care and in-vitro diagnostic products on its proprietary nanowire biosensor platform. The NanoDx™ System provides measurement of sample analytes in minutes from a variety of sample types. It utilizes a patented nano-sensor design that includes an array of highly sensitive biosensors that can be functionalized with an array of proteins that bind with target biomarkers. The unique size and electrical properties of the nano biosensors allow detection of biomarkers and proteins in a simple point-of-care system.

FDA has not yet reviewed this product and it has not been cleared. Currently for research use only.

about:
CEO and Co-Founder of the biotech startup Protein Capture Science focused on fundamentally changing how protein purification is performed by introducing single and scalable intein-based platform technology. Purified target proteins are tagless allowing on rapid seamless and streamlined proteins' development. This is a platform technology which is currently used in labs to purify non-mAbs including COVID-19 spike protein and filgrastim for research use. The goal of this platform technology is to bridge R&D and large-scale manufacturing. 

This technology is now available to purchase as the iCapTag platform. We have 1 mL and 5 mL FPLC cartridges/columns and 10 mL and 25 mL bottles containing bulk resin. A custom volume of bulk resin is available as well. Please contact us if you are interested to learn more about it. Currently our products are for research use only. current_position_description:
Commercialization of the iCapTag platform technology for protein purification to enable innovation in biotech-driven labs and manufacturing. This breakthrough technology results in tagless proteins. No proteases are required.

company_description:
Unmet Need:
There is an unmet need for a single-column universal platform method to reliably purify tagless recombinant proteins. Affinity tag methods followed by tag removal although commonly used are unreliable complex lead to productivity losses in research and are impractical for biopharmaceutical production.

Solution:
The key capabilities and advantages of the novel technology for protein purification provided by Protein Capture Science are:
1. Provides tagless purified proteins via tag self-cleavage during affinity purification
2. Gentle cleaving based on small pH change
3. Reproducible purity and recovery of target
4. Tunable target-independent cleavage rate
5. Scalable from laboratory to manufacturing
6. Simple and inexpensive buffer requirements
7. Opportunity to merge multi-platform processes into one platform (from R&D to production)

Product:
Intein-based product is a resin containing proprietary intein-based fragment that allows traceless and tagless self-cleaving protein purification. Two forms of product are available to purchase:
1. A kit containing pre-packed column (1mL or 5 mL)
2. A bulk resin (10 mL to L scales)

company_description:
La Jolla Institute for Immunology (LJI)

Our breakthroughs in immunology make life without disease possible.

We are a non-profit medical research institute dedicated to increasing knowledge and improving human health through studies of the immune system. We solely focus on the immune system because it holds the power to prevent treat and ultimately eliminate disease extending healthy human life. At LJI we accelerate lifesaving discoveries by combining a uniquely agile research environment with cutting-edge technology—empowering our world-class scientists to aim higher and move faster.

What is immunology?

Immunology is a broad branch of biomedical science that covers the study of all aspects of the immune response. Harnessing the immune system defeated polio. At LJI we're wielding that same power with greater precision so that cancer autoimmune infectious and neurodegenerative diseases are no longer inevitable but simply gone. 

Where is LJI?

Founded in 1988 La Jolla Institute is located in the University of California San Diego's Science Research Park in a world-class facility covering 145 000 square feet. Our space provides an open laboratory setting that encourages LJI's highly collaborative research environment.

about: A Ali Yanik is an Associate Professor in the Jack Baskin School of Engineering at University of California Santa Cruz. His current research focuses on isolation and single cell analysis of Circulating Tumor Cells from human blood using optofluidic-nanoplasmonic platforms. His research interests include nanoplasmonic and metamaterial devices for ultrasensitive infrared spectroscopy of biomolecules/chemicals as well as high-throughput cost effective BioNEMS technologies for life sciences point-of-care diagnostics and global health. His expertise is in high-end nanolithography and bio-patterning as well as theory and engineering of nanophotonic devices. He received his Ph.D. degree in applied theoretical physics from Purdue University under the supervision of Prof. Supriyo Datta. During his PhD he studied spin dependent electron transport in low dimensional molecular/nano-electronic devices and introduced one of the most advanced quantum transport models (spin-NEGF) that exists today. Before joining to UCSC he was a senior research associate at BioMEMS Resource Center (Mehmet Toner Group) at Harvard University Medical School & Massachusetts General Hospital. He was also instrumental in the establishment of The Laboratory of Integrated Nanophotonics and Biosensing Systems at The Boston University Photonics Center. He has published more than 80 peer reviewed journal articles and conference proceedings. His scientific contributions have been featured in numerous technical magazines and by scientific organizations including National Academy of Science National Science Foundation Institute of Physics Nature Materials Physics World US News IBM Think Project SPIE News Biophotonics and Biomarkers & Diagnostic Breakthroughs.

And it shows. 

UC Santa Cruz's many accomplishments include: 
• The first to map the human genome and make it publicly available—for free forever 
• Key contributors to the discovery of the Higgs Boson 
• The birthplace of organic farming
• The center of the Dickens Universe
• A graduate gaming program that is one of the top 10 in the country
• Ranked second in the world for its research impact

This university is not to be underestimated.

The campus's unique character is reflected in its students'​ bold choice to elect the fabled Banana Slug as its mascot—with their determined bearing and bright personality it's hard to ignore Banana Slugs wherever they may roam.

And Banana Slug alumni are everywhere—a network 100 000-plus-strong comprised of respected scientists journalists social activists political and business leaders artists teachers entrepreneurs change-makers and much more. Banana Slugs are changing the world.

about:
Over the past 20+years my research has focused on the production genetic manipulation and directed differentiation of pluripotent stem cells (PSCs) to clinically relevant cell types. My role at BlueRock is to provide the strategic vision to advance our cell+gene platform the foundation of BlueRock’s cell therapies. I also help manage key alliances with Editas Medicine Universal Cells and Senti Biosciences and participate in other external collaborations. My teams perform early discovery work in collaboration with our cell therapy programs to deeply characterize the cell product and to help engineer the genome for augmented function.

Prior to BlueRock I spent ~16 years at Memorial Sloan Kettering first as a postdoc with Lorenz Studer (2002-2006) but later running my own lab the SKI Stem Cell Research Facility (2006-2017). I was responsible for writing funding proposals and grants overseeing ~12 million USD teaching PSC culture methods to more than 140 scientists and co-authoring over 30 publications. My work was among the earliest to use engineered nucleases to target specific genomic payload to predefined sites in human pluripotent stem cells first using ZFNs then later TALENs and CRISPR/Cas systems as the technology evolved. 

My team led the GMP manufacture of MSK-DA01 a cell therapy derived from human pluripotent stem cells as part of a NYSTEM-funded consortium led by Lorenz Studer (2013-2017). My lab executed multiple roles in developing DA01 including: raw material risk assessment and mitigation process and assay development drafting SOPs and batch records and manufacturing clinical batches in the CTCEF GMP facility at MSK. DA01 is now the lead product in BlueRock Therapeutics pipeline which led to my transition to BlueRock.
company_description: BlueRock Therapeutics is a clinical stage cell therapy company focused on creating cellular medicines to treat devastating diseases. We are harnessing the power of cell therapy to create a pipeline of new medicines for people suffering from neurological and ophthalmic diseases. Two of our novel investigational cell therapies one for the treatment of Parkinson's disease and one for the treatment of primary photoreceptor diseases are clinical stage programs. BlueRock was founded in 2016 as a joint venture of Versant Ventures and Leaps by Bayer the impact investing arm of Bayer AG that invests in paradigm-shifting breakthrough innovation. In late 2019 BlueRock became a wholly owned independently operated subsidiary of Bayer AG. Our culture is defined by the courage to persist regardless of the challenge the urgency to transform medicine and deliver hope integrity guided by mission and community-mindedness with the understanding that we are all part of something bigger than ourselves. For more information visit www.bluerocktx.com.

I have to be honest with you though. I knew nothing about mattresses and even less about sleep. In my mind sleep was a nuisance a nonessential activity. And going to bed at a decent hour was a punishment like when I did something terrible as a child. Yet there I was a product developer who loves to immerse myself in the products they build and the problems they help solve. So I started the only way I knew how research. Lots of it! 

I've developed relationships and learned from leading sleep scientists doctors researchers and industry experts during this time. I have tested and experimented with hundreds of products designed to improve sleep. Smart home devices wearables meditation and breathwork apps to name a few. Not to mention experiencing countless mattresses smart beds pillows and linen sets. I've even automated my entire home around sleep and built my bedroom into a personalized recovery room.

Dedicating these past years of my life to studying sleep wellness has provided me with a fresh new perspective on the power of sleep. I quickly learned sleep is not the end of the day. It's the beginning.

JD has become a respected authority on the subject. He is a member of the Global Wellness Institute and a keynote speaker for the luxury hotel and wellness industry.

JD’s journey of discovery and experimentation has led to a profound understanding of the importance of sleep in achieving a healthier and more balanced life.

Reveal Biosensor is pioneering a cutting-edge medical-grade wearable technology poised to become a go-to patient monitoring solution across various care platforms. This innovative monitor empowers advanced in-hospital medical applications and a multitude of virtual patient care scenarios. By delivering hospital-quality data it ensures healthcare providers have the most important information at their fingertips.

Our next-generation physiologic wearable monitor will introduce groundbreaking new digital biomarkers: pulse amplitude and breathing effort. It also indicates cellular hypoxic stress which is more responsive and sensitive than traditional pulse oximetry. These novel biomarkers hold the potential to better enable more timely interventions. This more accurate assessment of patient status will help to optimize hospital stay durations and avoid costly readmissions.

Reveal Biosensor's strategy includes licensing our technology solutions to both wearable and medical device companies catering to specific medical application areas. Furthermore we envision a future where we develop and license prototype monitors in various formats covering the entire spectrum of human monitoring from infancy to end-of-life as well as on-organ formats tailored for advanced medical therapies.

Reveal Biosensors currently has on-going co-development arrangements with two global strategic partners. We are validating these new digital biomarkers for wearable applications in critical care medicine and worker safety.

company_description:
Delve Health is a clinical trial execution partner focused on one critical gap: what happens between visits.

While most platforms capture data Delve ensures that data is actually collected—consistently reliably and at scale. Through a unified model that combines eCOA/ePRO wearable integration and Concierge-as-a-Service™ we actively manage patient compliance engagement and data integrity across the full study lifecycle.

Our Clinical StudyPal™ platform enables sponsors CROs and research sites to deploy digital endpoints remote assessments and hybrid trial models without adding operational burden. We support a wide range of therapeutic areas including cardiology oncology CNS dermatology metabolic disease and rare conditions—where long-term follow-up and adherence are critical to success.

What makes Delve different is execution ownership.

At the core of this is our Concierge-as-a-Service™ model—a dedicated human layer assigned to every patient. This is not traditional support. Our protocol-trained multilingual concierge team actively engages patients reinforces study requirements resolves issues in real time and closes the loop on missed activities. Where technology can remind humans ensure follow-through.

We don't rely on passive dashboards. Our platform and concierge team work together to detect early signs of drift—missed diaries declining device use disengagement—and intervene before they impact endpoints. This results in consistently high performance across studies including >92% ePRO completion and >95% device compliance.

Delve consolidates what is typically fragmented across multiple vendors—eCOA wearables engagement and support—into a single coordinated solution. This reduces complexity improves visibility and strengthens dataset integrity.

Whether supporting post-market studies decentralized trials or real-world evidence programs Delve ensures that execution matches protocol intent.

company_description:
Welcome to the U.S. Research Laboratory LinkedIn page.

The U.S. Naval Research Laboratory (NRL) conducts a broad spectrum of cutting-edge scientific research with immediate and long-range applications for the Department of the Navy the Defense Department and the nation.

Founded July 2 1923 NRL is a scientific and engineering command dedicated to research that drives innovative advances for the U.S. Navy and the U.S. Marine Corps. NRL is the Department of the Navy's corporate laboratory and reports to the Chief of Naval Research. As the Navy's corporate laboratory NRL is the principal in-house component in the Office of Naval Research's (ONR) effort to meet its science and technology responsibilities.

NRL has had a long and fruitful relationship with industry as a collaborator contractor and through Cooperative Research and Development Agreements (CRADAs). NRL values this linkage and continues to develop it. NRL is an important link in the Navy Research Development and Acquisition (RD&A) chain. Through NRL the Navy has direct ties with sources of fundamental ideas in industry and the academic community throughout the world and provides an effective coupling point to the R&D chain for ONR.

NRL is a Navy Working Capital Fund activity which means it is not a line-item in the U.S. Federal Budget. Instead of direct funding from Congress all costs including overhead are redeemed through sponsor-funded research projects. 

For more information please visit the official NRL website at http://www.nrl.navy.mil/. 

If you have a media query please contact the NRL public affairs at 202-480-3746 or nrlpao@us.navy.mil.

Founded in 1810 the Yale School of Medicine is a world-renowned center for biomedical research education and advanced health care.

Among its divisions are one of the nation's oldest schools of public health and the internationally recognized Child Study Center founded in 1911. Its Yale Cancer Center is one of 41 comprehensive cancer centers designated by the National Cancer Institute and the school was one of the first 12 institutions to launch the Clinical and Translational Science Awards program in 2006. Its 33 academic departments include 10 in the basic sciences 18 in clinical fields and 5 in public health.

Affiliated institutions include the 1 541-bed Yale-New Haven Hospital—flagship of the Yale New Haven Health System and one of the largest hospitals in the United States since acquisition of the Saint Raphael campus—and the Connecticut Mental Health Center Pierce Laboratory and VA Connecticut Healthcare System in nearby West Haven.

The School of Medicine consistently ranks among the handful of leading recipients of research funding from the National Institutes of Health and other organizations supporting the biomedical sciences and belongs to medical organizations including the Association of American Medical Colleges (AAMC) and the Association of Academic Health Centers (AAHC).

The school's unique curriculum known as the Yale system of medical education promotes teaching in small seminar conference and tutorial settings and requires student self-evaluation independent thinking and investigation.

Since 1839 Yale has required that each student complete a thesis based on original research prior to graduation. Graduates of the school have gone on to significant leadership positions in virtually every medical field as well as many non-medical areas.

We asked ourselves what if coffee could be even better?

Instead of caffeine our coffee contains Px a caffeine derivative your body naturally makes after you consume caffeine. 

Just like caffeine Px wakes you up and reduces fatigue. But unlike caffeine Px won’t leave you feeling jittery wired or over-caffeinated. And if that wasn’t good enough Px is also cleared from your body faster than caffeine so you can drink Px coffee later in the day without worrying about it affecting your sleep at night.

company_description:
At Alkermes we apply our deep neuroscience expertise to develop medicines designed to help people living with complex and difficult-to-treat psychiatric and neurological disorders including addiction serious mental illness and sleep disorders. With our portfolio of marketed medicines and investigational pipeline candidates we seek to make a meaningful difference the way people manage these chronic diseases. Our patient-inspired science integrated research strategy sophisticated development capabilities and specialized commercial infrastructure enable us to pursue the development and commercialization of innovative new medicines including those for people with conditions that have traditionally been overlooked or stigmatized. Our greatest strengths at Alkermes are our people and our inherent empathy for patients families and caregivers. Neurological and psychiatric disorders strike people at the core of who they are and impact many facets of their lives. We work with urgency to develop new treatment options for people living with these conditions. 

Our core values also guide and define our culture and contribute to our recognition as an employer of choice in the biotech industry.

In 2025 Alkermes was proud to be recognized as:
- A Best Workplace in BioTech and Pharmaceuticals by Fortune Magazine 
- A Top Place to Work in Massachusetts by the Boston Globe 
- A Best Place to Work in Ireland by the Sunday Times Ireland and
- A Best Place to Work in Greater Cincinnati by the Cincinnati Business Courier 

Alkermes is headquartered in Ireland and also has a corporate office and research and development center in Massachusetts and a manufacturing facility in Ohio.
